hints.Exi only: There are two finite verbs: 'Exi' (go out!) and 'sum' (I am)
hints.sum only: 'sum' is a finite verb, but so is 'Exi' (imperative of 'exire')
hints.peccator and sum: 'peccator' is a noun (sinner), not a verb — the verbs are 'Exi' and 'sum'

hints.to exist: 'to exist' is 'existere' — 'Exi' comes from 'exire' (to go out)
hints.to examine: 'to examine' is 'examinare' — 'Exi' comes from 'exire' (to go out)
hints.to be: 'to be' is 'esse' — 'Exi' comes from 'exire' (to go out)

hints.present indicative: Present indicative would be 'exit' — 'Exi' is a command
hints.perfect indicative: Perfect indicative would be 'exiit' — 'Exi' is the imperative
hints.subjunctive: Subjunctive would be 'exeat' — 'Exi' is a direct command (imperative)

hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are principal parts of 'eo' (to go) — 'exeo' is the compound with 'ex-'
hints.exigo, exigere, exegi, exactum: These are principal parts of 'exigo' (to drive out/demand)
hints.exerceo, exercere, exercui, exercitum: These are principal parts of 'exerceo' (to train/exercise)

hints.perfect: Perfect of 'esse' 1st person is 'fui' — 'sum' is present
hints.imperfect: Imperfect 1st person would be 'eram' — 'sum' is present
hints.future: Future 1st person would be 'ero' — 'sum' is present

hints.sumo, sumere, sumpsi, sumptum: These are principal parts of 'sumo' (to take up)
hints.eo, ire, ivi, itum: These are principal parts of 'eo' (to go)
hints.do, dare, dedi, datum: These are principal parts of 'do' (to give)

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'hominem' — 'homo' is nominative (predicate: I am a sinful man)
hints.vocative: Vocative 'homo' is the same form but this is not an address — it's a predicate nominative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'hominis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'hominis' ends in -is

hints.accusative: Accusative would be 'peccatorem' — 'peccator' is nominative, modifying 'homo'
hints.genitive: Genitive would be 'peccatoris' — 'peccator' is nominative here
hints.vocative: Vocative would also be 'peccator' but this is a predicate, not an address

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'peccatoris' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'peccatoris' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -us — 'peccatoris' ends in -is

translation: Depart from me, for I am a sinful man
